steps.title: Identify the causal claim being made
steps.content:
The passage states that Aburto-Oropeza “attribute[s] this difference [in trophic composition] to the two reefs' difference in regulatory status” — arguing that protection causes higher percentages of top-level species.

To support this causal attribution, we need evidence of the mechanism linking protection to trophic composition.

steps.title: Find the mechanism linking regulatory status to trophic composition
steps.content:
Choice D reveals it: fishers disproportionately remove top-level species.

If fishers preferentially target top predators, the unprotected reef (subject to fishing) would have fewer of them by percentage, while the protected reef would preserve them — matching the observed 34% vs 10% difference.

The other three address total biomass variations, access costs, or species composition, none establishing the causal link between protection and trophic structure.

That is the correct answer.
